+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. This growth trajectory is largely attributed to a record order book and a 9 percent increase in underlying earnings per share, which reached 47.6 pence.On 11th March 2026, the construction group revealed that its top-line growth was significantly supported by the increasing demand for power transmission in the UK and a surge in building projects in the US. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. The expansion of infrastructure projects, particularly in power transmission, has provided a solid foundation for growth, enabling Balfour Beatty to solidify its position in the competitive construction landscape.Market analysts remain optimistic about Balfour Beatty’s future prospects, considering its substantial order book which continues to grow amidst a backdrop of increasing infrastructure investment. The company’s strategic initiatives to enhance operational margins and deliver projects on time have positioned it well for sustained growth in the coming quarters. Investors are likely to monitor Balfour Beatty’s performance closely as it seeks to leverage its strengths in both the UK and US markets.As Balfour Beatty continues to capitalize on the opportunities presented by the expanding power grid and overall construction demand, it remains crucial for stakeholders to assess the potential impacts of rising costs and project management challenges. Overall, the company’s recent performance indicates a strong recovery trajectory, making it a noteworthy player in the construction sector as it heads into the next fiscal year.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INNSBROOK OFFICE WITH LOCAL SUPPORT

DPR Construction, a leading regional construction firm, is set to expand its office space in Innsbrook by 9,100 square feet. The announcement comes as part of a strategic move to accommodate the company's growing workforce and enhance its operational capabilities. The expansion is supported by local incentives from Henrico County, which aims to attract and retain businesses in the area. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The new office space will allow DPR Construction to bring together teams and resources, fostering collaboration and innovation within the company. This growth is expected to create additional job opportunities in the region, further contributing to Henrico's economic development goals. Local officials have expressed optimism about the project, viewing it as a significant investment in the community's future.DPR Construction has been a prominent player in the construction industry, known for its commitment to quality and sustainability. The company's decision to expand its presence in Innsbrook reflects its confidence in the local market and the potential for continued growth. The support from Henrico County is part of broader efforts to enhance the business environment, making it easier for companies to invest and thrive.As part of the expansion, DPR Construction plans to implement modern design features and sustainable practices in the new office layout. This aligns with the company's mission to lead in sustainable construction practices while also providing a conducive work environment for its employees. The expansion is anticipated to be completed within the next year, further solidifying DPR Construction's commitment to the region.In addition to the expansion, DPR Construction has been actively involved in various community initiatives, underscoring its dedication to corporate social responsibility. As the company prepares for this new chapter, stakeholders are keenly watching how this expansion will impact both the firm and the local economy.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION MARKS KEY MILESTONE WITH ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER

FREDERICK, MD — Turner Construction Company celebrated the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, marking the completion of the structural phase for the 72-megawatt facility. This significant milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after breaking ground, showcasing the effectiveness of early planning and strong collaboration among the project team.To date, the project has seen over 80,000 craft hours logged, with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing to its progress. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," stated Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."The Aligned IAD-06 Data Center spans 450,000 square feet and is situated on a 75-acre campus designed to meet the increasing digital infrastructure demands of the region. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. With the structural phase completed, the focus will shift to the interior fit-out and operational readiness of the facility, further solidifying its role in enhancing the region's digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-3X53-S4C5-735G-00000-00Robo.ai CEO Issues Letter to Investors on 2025 Financial Results: Company Reaching Inflection Point with Positive Cash Flow and Business Order Delivery2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:14ZLexisNexisPRESS RELEASESRobo.ai CEO Issues Letter to Investors on 2025 Financial Results: Company Reaching Inflection Point with Positive Cash Flow and Business Order DeliveryStaff Writer

Robo.ai CEO Issues Letter to Investors on 2025 Financial Results: Company Reaching Inflection Point with Positive Cash Flow and Business Order DeliveryPR NewswireDUBAI, UAE, May 1, 2026

DUBAI, UAE, May 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Robo.ai Inc. (NASDAQ: AIIO) recently filed its annual financial report for the year 2025. Benjamin Zhai, the Chief Executive Officer, issued a letter to investors and strategic partners detailing the company's strategic reorganization and operational progress since the new management team assumed their roles in May 2025.In the AI software segment, the company is advancing its partnership with DaBoss, a Silicon Valley-based AI model data service provider. Operations for intelligent data training and collection are underway across multiple locations, aiming to deliver tens of thousands of hours of real-world interaction data and systematically realize commercial value. Additionally, Robo.ai is exploring strategic partnership opportunities with leading technology companies in the AI data application sector to address technical challenges and costs associated with large-scale data storage, transmission, and processing.Regarding smart hardware, the company provided an operational update on April 10, 2026, confirming the production and initial delivery of commercial validation vehicles from its subsidiary, Robus.

The initial batch of vehicles has passed client acceptance, and a second batch was successfully delivered and accepted in the South Asian market this week. Management views the initial fulfillment of these orders as validation of the commercial feasibility of its smart hardware strategy, and the company plans to continue allocating resources to meet global market demand.The company continues to invest in its "Machine Economy" strategy, which seeks to integrate artificial intelligence, robotics, and blockchain technology with physical assets in the real world. Following his participation in the Web3 Festival in Hong Kong, the CEO characterized this technological convergence as an emerging "R2R" (Robot World to Real World) business model, drawing parallels to the booming of "O2O" business model (online-to-offline integration) around 10 years ago. As part of this ecosystem, Arkreen, a Singaporean blockchain company within Robo.ai's investment portfolio, deployed a distributed solar project named eCandle in Africa this month. This initiative establishes an innovative digital payment infrastructure for the machine economy while successfully integrating green energy, digital assets, and social responsibilities.Addressing the 2025 financial results, management noted that the reported net loss was primarily driven by non-cash items and legacy matters. These included considerable amount of non-cash expenses related to shares issuance as well as impairment and liability provisions. Despite these accounting impacts, overall net cash flow turned positive as a result of cost controls and operational optimizations implemented in recent quarters, and the management considers this a critical positive inflection point. As a result of recent divestiture of a specific Cayman-based subsidiary, it is expected to yield a potential asset disposal gain of approximately $60 million.As a potential strategic investment target for Robo.ai, Shanghai Jidu Auto formally entered the judicial restructuring phase on April 29, 2026, following a pre-restructuring process initiated last year. Robo.ai's potential investment is intended to secure quality technology assets and strengthen its foundational capabilities in the smart mobility sector.Looking ahead to 2026, management acknowledges macroeconomic challenges stemming from geopolitical conditions in the Middle East, but notes that the region's increasing demand for independent supply chains, international capital, and technology presents ongoing operational opportunities that align with Robo.ai's globalized resources.Benjamin reiterates the management team's commitment to and confidence in the transformation of Robo.ai.About Robo.ai Inc.Robo.ai Inc. (NASDAQ: AIIO) is a technology company dedicated to building a leading global artificial intelligence machine economy platform. Its mission is to integrate "AI Software, Intelligent Hardware, and Smart Assets" to construct a unified AI operating system and an ecosystem empowered by blockchain, pioneering an intelligent future.Safe Harbor StatementThis press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Actual results may differ materially from those anticipated; for further details, please refer to the Company's filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.Website: www.roboai.io View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/roboai-ceo-issues-letter-to-investors-on-2025-financial-results-company-reaching-inflection-point-with-positive-cash-flow-and-business-order-delivery-302760017.htmlSOURCE Robo.ai Inc.

urn:contentItem:6JGK-4Y83-RSP9-70SW-00000-00Why this Oregon utility is betting big on California's energy market 2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:14ZLexisNexisBUSINESSWhy this Oregon utility is betting big on California's energy market Portland-based PacifiCorp is the first utility outside California to participate in the market, which pools generation and transmission assets to reduce costs and improve reliability.Ted Sickinger

PacifiCorp on Friday became the first utility outside California to join a new wholesale energy market, a move it says will generate substantial savings for customers, greater reliability and reduced greenhouse gas emissions.

The Portland-based utility, Oregon's second largest, joined the California Independent System Operator's extended day-ahead market, or EDAM, which went live Friday. CAISO is a non-profit that manages the high voltage grid for 80% of California and part of Nevada, and is now extending participation in its day-ahead market to utilities outside its borders.

In effect, PacifiCorp is pooling its generation and transmission assets with other western utilities and allowing CAISO to determine how to optimize the flow of power to meet each utility's forecast demand with the lowest cost power and transmission.

PacifiCorp is the first utility outside California to go live in the market, which currently includes that state's biggest investor-owned utilities: Southern California Edison, Pacific Gas & Electric and San Diego Gas & Electric.

PacifiCorp has a small number of customers in California but has not previously participated in the day-ahead market. It's a key player in its expansion, however, because it operates in six states and owns the largest privately held transmission system in the western United States. Other utilities are expected to follow suit, which would increase the market's efficiency, according to Mike Wilding, vice president of energy supply management at PacifiCorp.

"These organized markets are the best tool that we have available to control our costs for our customers," Wilding said in an interview. "The way they do that is they leverage larger footprints, a larger pool of generation and more transmission connectivity than PacifiCorp has on its own, which now means you can move electricity where it needs to go more effectively and more efficiently."

Portland General Electric has pledged to join the market in October, and other utilities in California and New Mexico are slated to join in 2027. Utilities in Idaho and Nevada are also evaluating joining.

Regionwide coordination of energy markets has been on the table for decades but has been a bugaboo for policy makers and utilities in the Northwest. Unlike the grid in the East and Midwest, the transmission system in the West is highly fragmented into individual utility fiefdoms.

But the need for better coordination and resource sharing has become more acute due to surging industrial energy demand, forecast supply shortfalls that could undermine reliability, and rising prices.

PacifiCorp's modeling shows EDAM could eventually save its customers $300 million annually. In addition, Wilding said the market will free up transmission capacity and boost system reliability by enabling regions with excess power to assist other areas experiencing extreme weather. And it will support greenhouse gas reductions by managing variations in output of renewable energy sources, like wind and solar, across a wider area.

The day-ahead market builds on the success of an energy imbalance market that CAISO launched in 2014. PacificCorp was also the first to join that market, which gives utilities access to energy to manage short term supply and demand imbalances. It spans 11 western states and has generated over $8.6 billion in benefits for participants since it launched.

One big player that won't be joining EDAM is the Bonneville Power Administration, which sells power from 31 hydroelectric dams and a nuclear plant, and which operates three quarters of the high voltage grid in the Northwest. Under pressure from customers leery of giving California too much control over the region's power grid, BPA last year made the controversial decision to join Markets+, a separate wholesale market operated by the Southwest Power Pool and headquartered in Arkansas.

At the time, BPA said it preferred the governance structure and reliability program of Markets+, despite studies showing more substantial savings from joining EDAM.

The federal agency's decision is being challenged in the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals by a coalition of environmental and consumer groups. They contend the decision will drive up energy bills in the region, undermine reliability and decrease access to renewables. The groups say the decision violates BPA's obligations under the Northwest Power Act to ensure cost effective and reliable power for the Northwest and protect fish and wildlife in the Columbia River Basin.

The California Independent System Operator has made significant changes to its own governance structure to accommodate expansion of the day-ahead market, transitioning to a new decision-making authority and laying the plans for a new regional organization.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-3HK3-RX4C-P4SH-00000-00TDK Unveils SensorStage Evaluation Platform to Enhance IMU Development2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:14ZLexisNexisTDK Unveils SensorStage Evaluation Platform to Enhance IMU Development

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 10:32 EDT

TDK Corporation has launched its latest innovation, the SensorStage Evaluation Platform, designed to streamline the development process for the company's SmartMotion Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs). This comprehensive tool aims to bridge the gap between basic graphical user interfaces and custom test environments, thereby enhancing the efficiency of developers. As of 6th May 2026, TDK's SensorStage is positioned to simplify the workflow with advanced visual analytics and automated scripting capabilities.

The SensorStage platform also introduces SensorGPT, a data synthesis technology that enables developers to create and manage sensor data at scale. This advancement is particularly significant for addressing the limitations of data pipelines, which often hinder the development of intelligent systems at the edge. By leveraging generative AI techniques, SensorGPT allows for the creation of synthetic data from real-world measurements, thereby enhancing the scalability, performance, and reliability of applications such as physical AI and audio AI.

At the Sensors Converge 2026 event in Santa Clara, California, TDK is showcasing its latest offerings, including MEMS, TMR, and software solutions from its InvenSense division. These innovations are geared toward next-generation applications, with a focus on intelligent sensing and context awareness. The SensorStage platform, in particular, is designed to significantly reduce development time for Edge AI models, making it a vital tool for developers seeking to bring new products to market more rapidly.

Rosa Chow, Software Vice President at InvenSense, emphasized the importance of the SensorStage platform in simplifying inertial sensor development, which has traditionally required extensive expertise. According to Chow, the platform allows customers to tailor workflows to their specific needs, facilitating rapid iteration and optimization. This capability is crucial for businesses aiming to enhance their time-to-market for complex designs.

Currently, the SensorStage platform is compatible with TDK's InvenSense SmartMotion IMUs, specifically the ICM-456xx and ICM-426xx models, with plans for future compatibility with additional MEMS sensor solutions. The platform's user-friendly design, combined with its advanced analytical capabilities, is expected to set a new standard in sensor evaluation and development, positioning TDK Corporation as a leader in the sensor technology space.

SECTION 1 TDK ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 TDK Activities

Activities

TDK Corporation, a multinational electronics company headquartered in Japan, specializes in the design, development, and manufacturing of advanced electronic components and systems. Its core business activities encompass passive components such as capacitors and inductors, magnetic application products including HDD heads and magnets, and energy devices like batteries and power supplies. These products serve diverse sectors, including consumer electronics, automotive, and industrial applications. The company's fastest-growing segments include sensors for autonomous vehicles and Internet of Things (IoT) devices, driven by rising demand for electrification and connectivity, as well as energy storage solutions for renewable energy and electric vehicles. Through innovation and strategic expansions, TDK continues to strengthen its position in the global electronics market. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 9th largest Electrical component & equipment company by market capitalisation.

Nuvve NASDAQ:NVVE - Get Free Report and Greenkraft OTCMKTS:GKIT - Get Free Report are both small-cap auto/tires/trucks companies, but which is the better stock? We will compare the two companies based on the strength of their dividends, institutional ownership, analyst recommendations, profitability, valuation, earnings and risk.

Analyst Ratings

This is a breakdown of current ratings for Nuvve and Greenkraft, as provided by MarketBeat.

Volatility and Risk

Nuvve has a beta of 1.44, meaning that its stock price is 44% more volatile than the S&P 500. Comparatively, Greenkraft has a beta of 206.36, meaning that its stock price is 20,536% more volatile than the S&P 500.

Insider & Institutional Ownership

19.0% of Nuvve shares are held by institutional investors. 17.5% of Nuvve shares are held by insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that hedge funds, endowments and large money managers believe a stock will outperform the market over the long term.

Greenkraft has lower revenue, but higher earnings than Nuvve.

Summary

Greenkraft beats Nuvve on 5 of the 9 factors compared between the two stocks.

About Nuvve Get Free Report

Nuvve Holding Corp., a green energy technology company, provides commercial vehicle-to-grid V2G technology platform in the United States, the United Kingdom, France, and Denmark. The company offers Grid Integrated Vehicle platform, which enables electric vehicle EV batteries to store and resell unused energy back to the local electric grid and provide other grid services, as well as allows EV owners to meet the energy demands of individual vehicles and entire fleets. Its V2G technology also links EV batteries into a virtual power plant that sells excess power to utility companies or utilizes saved power to reduce building energy peak consumption. In addition, the company offers networked charging stations, infrastructure, software, professional services, support, monitoring, and parts and labor warranties required to run electric vehicle fleets. It serves its products to owners/operators of light duty fleets, heavy duty fleets, automotive manufacturers, charge point operators, and strategic partners. The company was founded in 2010 and is headquartered in San Diego, California.

About Greenkraft Get Free Report

Greenkraft, Inc. manufactures and distributes automotive products for fleet operators, cities, and businesses in the United States. The company offers commercial forward cabin trucks that run on alternative fuels, such as compressed natural gas or liquefied propane gas. It also manufactures and sells alternative fuel systems; and converts petroleum-based fuel vehicles to run on alternative fuels, such as CNG or LPG. The company has strategic partnership with CEE, LLC and G&K Automotive Conversion Inc. for research and development activities, as well as to test its engines prior to applying for the CARB and EPA certifications. Greenkraft, Inc. was founded in 2006 and is headquartered in Santa Ana, California.

SECTION 1: BIOGRAPHY: MARC HAUGEN, DIRECTOR

Mr. Haugen joined the Board in August 2017. Mr. Haugen currently serves as chief executive officer of Lockwood Industries LLC (dba Fralock), an engineered specialty materials solutions company and portfolio company of Arsenal Capital Partners, since February 2020. Previously, Mr. Haugen was chief operating officer of Kateeva, Inc., a leading provider of OLED mass-production equipment solutions, from January 2018 to February 2020. In 2017, Mr. Haugen established Following Seas Ventures LLC for consulting and private investment, primarily focused on equipment and solutions for sustainability and renewable energy. From 2016 to 2017, Mr. Haugen was executive vice president of engineering and operations at Cepheid Inc., a leading molecular diagnostics company, a position he held until its acquisition by the Danaher Corporation. From 2013 to 2016, Mr. Haugen was group vice president, worldwide operations and supply chain at Applied Materials, Inc. Prior to this, Mr. Haugen held various general management, product, manufacturing, and business operations roles at Lam Research Corporation through the corporate vice president level, from 1998 to 2013. Mr. Haugen's service at Applied Materials and Lam Research included many years of international assignments at the senior executive level in Asia and Europe. Mr. Haugen began his business career at Applied Materials from 1991 to 1998 after serving four years as an officer in the U.S. Navy on surface combatant ships. Mr. Haugen received an M.B.A. from the University of California Los Angeles and the National University of Singapore and a B.S. in industrial and systems engineering from the University of Southern California.

APPENDIX II STOCK IDENTIFIERS

SECTION 1 CORPORATE PROFILE

1.1 Activities

Oklo Inc. is a pioneering company in the nuclear energy sector, specializing in the development and deployment of advanced fission reactors to deliver safe, carbon-free power. Headquartered in the United States and listed on the New York Stock Exchange, the company focuses on innovative technologies such as fast reactors and fuel recycling, which enhance energy efficiency and minimize nuclear waste. Its fastest-growing segments include the design and commercialization of small modular reactors (SMRs), which are gaining momentum due to their compact size, scalability, and ability to integrate with renewable energy systems. Oklo is also expanding through strategic partnerships with utilities and government entities, accelerating regulatory approvals and deployment efforts. This positions the company as a key contributor to the global shift toward sustainable energy, addressing rising demands for reliable, low-emission power solutions in data centers, industrial operations, and beyond.

SECTION 2 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES, TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

2.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 1, Source: Oklo Inc., 132 words

May 13: Oklo Publishes First Quarter 2026 Financial Results and Business Update

SANTA CLARA, Calif.----Oklo Inc. (NYSE: OKLO) (Oklo or the Company), an advanced nuclear technology company, today published its financial results and business update for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026.

Oklo will host a conference call today, May 12, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. Pacific Time / 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time. Jacob DeWitte, Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer, and Craig Bealmear, Chief Financial Officer, will speak on the call.

A webcast of the call can be accessed by visiting the Events & Presentations section of the Companys investor relations website. An archive of the webcast will be available shortly after the conclusion of the event and will be available for 12 months.

2.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 101 words

Aurora-INL Groundbreaking: Construction Begins on Flagship 75-MWe Fast Reactor

Oklo has commenced physical construction of its 75-MWe Aurora fast reactor at Idaho National Laboratory (INL). This milestone, supported by a 2019 site use permit and DOE authorization, transitions the project from design to development. Kiewit Nuclear Solutions will lead construction. The project utilizes metallic HALEU fuel derived from the historic EBR-II reactor, with fuel fabrication occurring at INL. While originally slated for a 2027-2028 launch, participation in the DOE's Reactor Pilot Program could accelerate criticality to a July 2026 target. This project represents a significant scale-up from a previously denied 1.5-MW prototype, demonstrating technological maturation and a modernized regulatory strategy.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 98 words

Tennessee Advanced Fuel Center Announcement: $1.7 Billion Investment in Oak Ridge Fuel Recycling Facility

Oklo announced a $1.68 billion investment to build an advanced nuclear fuel center in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, including the first U.S. privately funded commercial fuel recycling facility. Set to begin production in the early 2030s, the center will produce metallic HALEU fuel for Aurora reactors by recycling used fuel from conventional reactors. The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) has expressed interest in a partnership, potentially creating a closed-loop fuel cycle. This initiative aligns with recent policy shifts away from the 1977 moratorium on reprocessing and is crucial for establishing the fuel supply infrastructure for Oklo's planned 14-gigawatt reactor fleet.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

Siemens Energy Binding Contract: Power Conversion System for Aurora Powerhouse

Oklo has executed a binding contract with Siemens Energy for the design and delivery of the power conversion system for the Aurora-INL reactor. The agreement covers an SST-600 steam turbine and an SGen-100A generator, which will interface with a supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle. This move de-risks the project by securing long-lead components from an established global manufacturer. The design leverages proven, commercially available turbomachinery to reduce technical risk and shorten timelines. The contract is a key step in solidifying the supply chain for Oklo's broader 14-gigawatt commercial pipeline.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 92 words

DOE Nuclear Reactor Pilot Program Selection: Three Projects Fast-Tracked for Aurora and Atomic Alchemy

The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) selected Oklo for three projects in its new Reactor Pilot Program: the Aurora-INL reactor, Atomic Alchemy's VIPR radioisotope reactor, and a third project for fuel development. This provides an accelerated regulatory pathway under DOE authorization, with a target for criticality by July 2026. Oklo's subsidiary, Atomic Alchemy, was also selected for the Advanced Nuclear Fuel Line Pilot Program. Oklo's unique position, including existing permits, allocated fuel, and advanced designs, has allowed it to progress faster than competitors within the program, validating its technology and regulatory strategy.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 91 words

NRC Pre-Application Readiness Assessment Completion: Regulatory Pathway Advancement

Oklo successfully completed the NRC's pre-application readiness assessment for Phase 1 of its combined license application (COLA) for the Aurora-INL project. The NRC affirmed Oklo's readiness to submit its formal application for siting and environmental aspects, identifying no significant regulatory gaps. This proactive engagement, building on years of interaction with the NRC, is expected to streamline the formal review process. Supported by the ADVANCE Act and reduced NRC fees, this milestone validates Oklo's licensing strategy and enhances investor confidence as the company prepares to submit the formal COLA later in 2025.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 82 words

newcleo Strategic Partnership with $2 Billion Transatlantic Investment Commitment

Oklo has formed a strategic partnership with European developer newcleo, securing a commitment of up to $2 billion in investment for U.S. fuel fabrication and manufacturing. The collaboration aims to advance a closed-loop fuel cycle and explore the disposition of surplus plutonium as a fuel source, transforming a liability into a valuable asset. This transatlantic cooperation enhances energy security and validates Oklo's vertically integrated fuel strategy. The agreement also builds upon previous collaborations and signals strong international confidence in Oklo's commercialization plans.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 85 words

Atomic Alchemy Acquisition Closure: Radioisotope Producer Strategic Addition

Oklo completed its acquisition of radioisotope producer Atomic Alchemy for $25 million in an all-stock transaction. Atomic Alchemy, now a subsidiary, is developing the VIPR reactor to produce medical and industrial radioisotopes, a market with high demand and constrained supply. This strategic move diversifies Oklo's revenue streams beyond electricity generation, secures a position in the domestic radioisotope supply chain, and leverages synergies in regulatory expertise and materials handling. The acquisition also positions Atomic Alchemy to benefit from accelerated authorization through the DOE Reactor Pilot Program.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) Memorandum of Understanding for Global Aurora Deployment

Oklo signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) to support the standard design and global deployment of the Aurora Powerhouse. KHNP, a world-leading nuclear builder, will contribute extensive expertise in reactor construction, supply chain development, and operational management. The partnership is critical for scaling up manufacturing and construction to meet Oklo's 14-gigawatt commercial pipeline commitments. The collaboration leverages KHNP's project delivery experience to accelerate Oklo's path to commercialization and sets the stage for future international market entry.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 81 words

$400 Million Equity Offering and $1.5 Billion At-The-Market Program: Capital Market Access and Financing Strategy

Oklo has significantly strengthened its financial position through a $400 million public equity offering and the establishment of a $1.5 billion at-the-market (ATM) program. These moves provide substantial capital and flexible access to funding for project development, manufacturing, and strategic investments. As of November 2025, the company's liquidity stood at approximately $2.7 billion, providing a multi-year runway that far exceeds its projected cash burn. This demonstrated market confidence eliminates near-term refinancing pressures and positions Oklo to execute its 14-gigawatt commercial pipeline.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 95 words

DOE Aurora Fuel Fabrication Facility (A3F) Nuclear Safety Design Agreement Approval: Accelerated Authorization Pathway

The DOE approved the Nuclear Safety Design Agreement (NSDA) for Oklo's Aurora Fuel Fabrication Facility (A3F) at Idaho National Laboratory. This approval, granted in just two weeks, authorizes the fabrication of metallic HALEU fuel for the Aurora-INL reactor and sets a precedent for the DOE's new accelerated fuel production framework. Oklo also expanded its collaboration with INL's operator to use the Aurora reactor for advanced fuel and materials research, integrating commercial operations with R&D. This milestone enables the transition from fuel design to production, a critical step in building a domestic advanced fuel supply chain.

2.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 278 words

Apr 27, 2026: X-Energy IPO Sets New Capital Markets Benchmark for Advanced Nuclear, Increasing Strategic Pressure on Oklo

Amazon-backed advanced nuclear reactor developer X-Energy priced its upsized initial public offering on April 23, 2026, raising approximately $1.02 billion by selling 44.3 million Class A shares at $23 each, well above the marketed range of $16 to $19, according to industry reports and the company's official announcement. Shares began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker "XE" on April 24, 2026.

The offering is anchored by significant commercial commitments. Amazon holds a binding agreement to procure up to 5 gigawatts of nuclear power by 2039, while Dow Inc. has contracted to source industrial process heat and power from an Xe-100 deployment at its Seadrift, Texas chemical complex.

The successful pricing comes amid intensifying electricity demand from hyperscale AI data centers and industrial reshoring, signaling that institutional capital markets now view small modular reactor technology as commercially fundable infrastructure.

Impact on Oklo Inc.: OKLO

Following this news, Oklo Inc. faces a recalibrated competitive landscape in advanced nuclear, where X-Energy's $1.02 billion raise establishes a fresh public-market reference point. Oklo, which uses a fast-spectrum sodium-cooled Aurora powerhouse design targeting smaller-scale baseload applications, now competes for hyperscaler and industrial customer contracts against an X-Energy that is significantly better-capitalized for fuel manufacturing and reactor deployment.

Both companies target similar customer archetypes, including data center operators, remote industrial sites, and utilities seeking 24/7 carbon-free power. Oklo's strategic differentiation lies in its build-own-operate model, where it sells power rather than reactors, contrasting with X-Energy's reactor-and-fuel sales approach. The IPO pressures Oklo to demonstrate accelerated NRC licensing progress and customer offtake commitments comparable to X-Energy's anchors.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 314 words

Apr 24, 2026: X-energy's $1.02 Billion Nasdaq Debut Introduces a Better-Capitalized Publicly Listed Rival to Oklo in the Small Modular Reactor Market

X-energy, Inc., a Rockville, Maryland-based developer of small modular reactor (SMR) technology and proprietary TRISO fuel, priced its upsized initial public offering at $23.00 per share on April 23, 2026. The company sold 44,254,659 shares of Class A common stock, raising approximately $1.02 billion in gross proceeds before underwriting discounts. Pricing came $4 above the top of the marketed $16.00-to-$19.00 range, and the deal was upsized from the 42.86 million shares originally filed. Underwriters received a 30-day option to purchase up to 6,638,198 additional shares.

Shares begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on April 24, 2026 under the ticker symbol "XE," with the offering expected to close on April 27, 2026. J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Jefferies, and Moelis & Company served as lead joint book-running managers.

The listing arrives as investor interest in advanced nuclear technology has accelerated amid rising electricity demand from data centers and industrial electrification, sharpening competitive dynamics across the advanced-reactor value chain.

Impact on Oklo: OKLO

Following this development, Oklo faces a publicly listed peer with over $1 billion of fresh equity capital competing for the same utility and hyperscale data-center customers Oklo targets with its Aurora powerhouse. While Oklo's fast-reactor design and build-own-operate model remain differentiated, X-energy's Xe-100 platform and integrated TRISO-X fuel franchise bring a competing value proposition, with extension into industrial process heat, to public markets at scale. The above-range pricing sets a higher capital-raising benchmark for future Oklo funding rounds and follow-on offerings.

Oklo will now compete for NRC licensing bandwidth, nuclear engineering talent, and Department of Energy cost-share awards against a rival pursuing parallel deployment milestones with Dow and Energy Northwest. Category validation is directionally favorable for Oklo's narrative, though the narrowed capital-access gap tightens competitive dynamics across commercial negotiations and customer qualification processes.

value.Title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK8-3TC3-SBJX-V3X9-00000-00INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:14ZLexisNexisINVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf Of Investors Of Primoris Services Corporation - PRIM

NEW YORK, May 14, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Pomerantz LLP is investigating claims on behalf of investors of Primoris Services Corporation ("Primoris" or the "Company") (NYSE: PRIM ). Such investors are advised to contact Danielle Peyton at [email protected] or 646-581-9980, ext. 7980.


The investigation concerns whether Primoris and certain of its officers and/or directors have engaged in securities fraud or other unlawful business practices.
[Click here for information about joining the class action]
On May 5, 2026, Primoris issued a press release reporting its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. Primoris reported results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million. Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects.
On this news, Primoris's stock price fell $101.69 per share, or 50.11%, to close at $101.23 per share on May 6, 2026.
Pomerantz LLP, with offices in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Tel Aviv, is acknowledged as one of the premier firms in the areas of corporate, securities, and antitrust class litigation. Founded by the late Abraham L. Pomerantz, known as the dean of the class action bar, Pomerantz pioneered the field of securities class actions. Today, more than 85 years later, Pomerantz continues in the tradition he established, fighting for the rights of the victims of securities fraud, breaches of fiduciary duty, and corporate misconduct. The Firm has recovered numerous multimillion-dollar damages awards on behalf of class members. See .
Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.
CONTACT:
Danielle Peyton
Pomerantz LLP

646-581-9980 ext. 7980
